message:
fix(agent): graceful error recovery during stream event processing

Replace the early-return `?` operator on stream event errors with
structured error handling that preserves partial assistant messages
and emits proper stop reasons.

Previously, when the SSE stream yielded an error mid-response (e.g.,
network interruption, provider-side timeout, malformed chunk), the
agent would propagate the error immediately via `event_result?`,
discarding any partial content already received and leaving the
message history in an inconsistent state.

Now the error path:
1. Checks if a partial assistant message was already being accumulated
   (`added_partial` flag) and recovers it from the message history
2. Falls back to a default empty AssistantMessage if no partial exists
3. Sets `stop_reason = StopReason::Error` and populates `error_message`
   with the stringified error for downstream diagnostics
4. Routes through `finalize_assistant_message` which handles Start
   event bookkeeping, ensuring the event stream remains well-formed
   regardless of when the error occurred in the stream lifecycle

This prevents silent content loss during transient provider errors
and gives callers a structured error signal they can retry or surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
